lobbying_activities: 1st July 2025: Delegation of ODG representatives met with Deputy Quaide to discuss the priorities for disabled people outlined in the ODG Pre-Budget 2026 Submission. - Virtual Meeting
24th July 2025:Delegation of ODG representatives met with Deputy McAuliffe to discuss the priorities for disabled people outlined in the ODG Pre-Budget 2026 Submission. - Virtual Meeting
31st July 2025: Delegation of ODG representatives met with Deputy Wall to discuss the priorities for disabled people outlined in the ODG Pre-Budget 2026 Submission. - Virtual Meeting
31st July 2025: Email sent to Deputy Wall following up on the meeting and inviting the Labour Party to meet with ODG representatives again in the led up to Budget 2026. - Email
8th July 2025: Delegation of ODG representatives met with Deputy O'Murchu to discuss the priorities for disabled people outlined in the ODG Pre-Budget 2026 Submission. - Virtual Meeting
July 2025: Engaged with Deputy McAuliffe and his office to arrange a briefing session for Fianna Fail Parliamentary Party in September 2025. - Email
June 2025: Emails exchanged with the Oireachtas Members providing information on arranging briefing session. - Email

May 1st 2025: Correspondence with Deputy Richard Boyd-Barrett's office regarding arranging a meeting to discuss a letter issued by the ODG outlining the priorities for disabled people that should be considered by the 34th Dail. - Email
May 2025: Emails exchanged with Minister Naughton and her office regarding sponsorship for the ODG to enter Leinster House for briefing meetings. - Email

records.specific_details: Annual National Household Travel Survey
records.intended_results: To reconsider proceeding with this outdated method, https://www.etenders.gov.ie/epps/cft/prepareViewCfTWS.do?resourceId=5530425 I propose leveraging anonymized mobile phone GPS tracking data combined with AI-driven analysis to create a more robust, scalable, and real-time solution for transport planning and management. ::Educate TDs on the near-future possibilities with AI traffic management, as mentioned during the media debate about Metrolink, highlighted by Dermot Desmond at an AI conference in Belfast https://www.irishtimes.com/ireland/dublin/2025/08/05/dublins-planned-metrolink-will-be-obsolete-because-of-artificial-intelligence-says-dermot-desmond/

To appear before the new AI, Transport and Infrastructure committees. - Phone call

records.specific_details: Request to Government to capitalise on the potential that Ireland's west coast offers for the development of a floating offshore wind sector.
records.intended_results: Ensure sufficient funding and resources are dedicated to developing the Floating Offshore Wind sector, capitalising on the high wind speeds along Ireland's west coart.::Ensure that public and private enterprise, research institutions and skilled workers across the Atlantic Economic Corridor are embedded in the offshore wind value chain by accelerating investment in enabling marine area plans and infrastructure.::Ensure that the grid is upgraded to support renewable energy connections and future-proof business growth.::Expand SEAI supports and energy efficient grants. ::Encourage green investment incentives / support regional climate adaptation funding / enhance tax breaks or grants to companies that are achieving their carbon neutrality.

records.specific_details: Seeking Government measures to support the continued recovery of Ireland's tourism and hospitality industry
records.intended_results: Restoration of the 9% rate of VAT for hospitality food services::Measures to improve cost competitiveness and avoid any further cost increases::For a review to be carried out of options for assisting hotels to develop employer-led staff accommodation across the sector::Targeted funding for a national hotel retrofitting scheme to reduce carbon footprint::Increased investment and support for tourism marketing both domestically and overseas.::Insurance reform and policy to avoid any additional increases in insurance costs::Increased investment in training and skills development within the sector::Robust enforcement of regulations around short term lettings::Additional resources for tourism economic research::Avoid the introduction of a tourist tax::Increased air access including removal of cap at Dublin airport

records.specific_details: Pre Budget Submission requesting targeted policy recommendations aimed at advancing Ireland's strategic priorities across reginal connectivity, climate action and balanced economic development.
records.intended_results: Prioritise investment in regional state-owned airports through the Regional Airports Programme. Strengthen regional connectivity and support climate action through the delivery of better transport connectivity. Support Regional Tourism and accelerate the renewables-based energy system by supporting the recommendations of the Shannon Estuary Economic Taskforce report.

records.intended_results: To brief political representatives on LGI’s policy priorities to support the decarbonisation of rural homes and businesses beyond the natural gas grid, emphasising the need for a ‘Just Transition’ in Ireland and the potential impact of the upcoming Renewable Heat Obligation Bill.

records.specific_details: Regnum Renewables and Ivan Yates (providing advisory services) briefed the West Clare MD councillors in advance of community engagement on a proposed wind farm near Lissycasey, Dehomad Wind Farm, and subsequently engaged with four Clare TDs to share information and request introductory meetings.
records.intended_results: The meeting and engagements aimed to provide an introduction to Regnum and the proposed Dehomad Wind Farm, ahead of community engagement, and to establish lines of communication with elected representatives in anticipation of future correspondence from the community.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Climate, Energy and the Environment

lobbying_activities: On 04/06/25 in the Kilrush Area Office, Ivan Yates and representatives from Regnum met with West Clare MD councillors to brief them on a proposed wind farm development near Lissycasey. Councillors in attendance included Dinny Gould, Rita McInerney, Ian Lynch, Michael Shannon, and Joe Killeen, with Shane Talty and Joe Garrihy joining remotely. The meeting was to introduce the project and to establish lines of communication in advance of engagement with local residents the following week. - Meeting

On 1 August 2025, Daragh Browne of Regnum emailed Minister of State Timmy Dooley (Departments of Agriculture, Food and the Marine; Climate, Environment and Energy) to provide information on the proposed Dehomad wind farm and request an introductory meeting. - Email

On 13/06/2025 June, Daragh Browne of Regnum emailed West Clare MD councillors Gabriel Keating and Bill Slattery, who had missed the meeting, to share the information discussed and offer to meet at a later date. - Email

On 13/08/25, Regnum issued emails to Deputies Cathal Crowe, Donna McGettigan, and Joe Cooney, sharing information on the Proposed Dehomad Wind Farm project and requesting introductory meetings. - Email

records.specific_details: Regnum Renewables and Ivan Yates (providing advisory services) briefed the West Clare MD councillors in advance of community engagement on a proposed wind farm near Lissycasey, Dehomad Wind Farm, and subsequently engaged with four Clare TDs to share information and request introductory meetings.
records.intended_results: The meeting and engagements aimed to provide an introduction to Regnum and the proposed Dehomad Wind Farm, ahead of community engagement, and to establish lines of communication with elected representatives in anticipation of future correspondence from the community.

records.specific_details: Correspondence regarding the delivery of Small Public Service Vehicle (SPSV) services in rural Ireland and the expansion of the Local Area Hackney licence to enhance local transport options.
records.intended_results: To highlight the challenges faced by Local Area Hackney drivers in delivering transport services to rural communities, and to secure meetings aimed at identifying practical solutions to support drivers and address these issues.

records.specific_details: ODG Pre-Budget Submission 2026
records.intended_results: Funding to support the key priorities for disabled people as outlined in the Oireachtas Disability Group's Pre-Budget Submission 2026 (available at: https://www.disability-federation.ie/assets/files/pdf/final_odg_pre-budget_2026_submission_1.pdf)

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Children, Equality, Disability, Integration and Youth

lobbying_activities: 4 virtual meetings held with various representatives to discuss the priorities for disabled people outlined in the ODG Pre-Budget Submission 2026: - 01/07/2025, delegation of ODG representatives met with Deputy Quaide. - 08/07/2025, delegation of ODG representatives met with Deputy O'Murchu. - 24/07/2025, delegation of ODG representatives met with Deputy McAuliffe. - 31/07/2025, delegation of ODG representatives met with Deputy Wall. - Virtual Meeting

Correspondence with Deputy Richard Boyd-Barrett's office in May to arrange a meeting to discuss a letter issued by the ODG priorities for disabled people that should be considered by the 34th Dáil. - Email
Email sent to Deputy Wall inviting the Labour Party to meet with ODG representatives again in the lead up to Budget 2026. - Email
Emails exchanged with Deputy McAuliffe and his office in July to arrange a briefing session for Fianna Fáil Parliamentary Party in September. - Email
Emails exchanged with Minister Naughton and her office in May regarding sponsorship for the ODG to enter Leinster House for briefing meetings. - Email
Emails exchanged with various Oireachtas Members providing information and to arrange briefing sessions. - Email

Meeting with Minister Moynihan and officials of NCSE, his policy advisor and Fianna Fail TD's, Albert Dolan, John Connolly and Senators Shane Curley and Anne Rabbitte to discuss: criteria for accessing SSLD Classes nationwide and the impact of new criteria; GLD classes for children who have Mild & Moderate learning disabilities; allocation of Special Classes - imbalance in allocation for children who have Down syndrome in comparison to other disabilities. - Meeting

records.specific_details: The Wheel hosted launch event for our Pre-Budget Submission 2026. It comprised a panel discussion with speakers from community and voluntary organisations and an overview of The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026. We invited Ministers, TDs and Senators from the most relevant departments and joint Oireachtas committees and several attended in person.
records.intended_results: To inform TDs and Senators about The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026, which includes issues such as sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.

records.specific_details: Youth Work policy
records.intended_results: Secure a meeting with Minister for Education and Youth Minister McEntee to discuss youth work and priorities for the youth work sector::Secure a meeting with new Chair of Oireachtas Committee on Education and Youth to discuss youth work and priorities for the youth work sector & ensure the Committee gives sufficient time to youth::Meetings with TDs and Senators to discuss youth work and priorities for the youth work sector::Secure meeting with members of Oireachtas Committee on Education and Youth to discuss youth work and priorities for the youth work sector & ensure the Committee gives sufficient time to youth::Oral Parliamentary Questions to be raised on youth work::Share Parliamentary question responses::Ensure that Department of Education and Youth officials were aware of priority areas of importance for the youth work sector::Provide wording for a potential Seanad PMB on youth work
lobbying_activities: Cliodhna O'Neill and Mary Cunningham - Virtual Meeting
Emails to Oireachtas Committee on Education and Youth Members; individual emails - Email

Louise O'Reilly TD; Senator Nicole Ryan; Ciaran Ahern TD; Senator Gareth Scahill; Peter Roche TD; Ryan O'Meara TD; Minister Helen Mc Entee, Bernie McNally & Cliodhna O'Neill; Shonagh NÍ Raghallaigh - Meeting
Text message from Mary Cunningham CEO to Malcolm Byrne TD - Informal communication

records.specific_details: Role of Young people in Oireachtas Committee Terms of Reference
records.intended_results: Ensure that Committees engage with young people to ensure their voices are heard in all aspects of the Committee's work.::Ensure Committees collaborate with youth organisations, advocates, and stakeholders that represent the interests of young people to gather insights and contributions to the Committee's discussions and initiatives.
lobbying_activities: Email to all Oireachtas Committee Chairs - Email

records.specific_details: Emails and meetings with government and opposition TDs to discuss funding for school libraries in Ireland.
records.intended_results: Government and opposition party support for sustained and annual funding for school libraries in primary and secondary schools in Ireland.

dpo_entries.public_body: Department of Public Expenditure, NDP Delivery and Reform

lobbying_activities: On the 11th of June, I met with Cathal Crowe in his capacity as Chair of the Oireachtas Education Committee to request a speaking slot before the Committee and discuss the importance of funding school libraries. On the 16th of June, I met with Jen Cummins in her capacity as Education Spokesperson for the Social Democrats to discuss support for school libraries. On the 4th of July, I met with Aodhán Ó Ríordáin (Labour MEP) on child and youth literacy support. - Meeting

On the 15th of May, I requested a meeting with Cathal Crowe to discuss the outcomes of our Reading for Joy Forum and book gifting application data. On the 7th of July, I shared our Children and Young People's Reading in Ireland report with Jen Cummins and Cathal Crowe, and on the 10th of July, I shared same with Paschal Donohoe, Jack Chambers, Helen McEntee to request consideration for school library funding in Budget 2026, with a follow-up request for a meeting with the latter on August 8th. - Email

records.specific_details: Update to members of Oireachtas on 221+ work
records.intended_results: Continued funding for 221+, Commitment & funding to extend services of 221+ to all women let down by the CervicalCheck screening programme, implement the AHR Act and push through Amendments bill, exclude 221+ member court payments from means testing, support gynaecological health, Implement Patient Voice Partner Policy, implement the 2018 Lymphoedema model of care, continued investment in Elimination of Cervical Cancer, Ringfenced funding for women’s health. Event sponsored by Marie Sherlock TD.
